publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11922288.
This collection contains 118 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 832 digital files.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid written in English.

Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:02

did.unittitle: American Historical Financial Documents Collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, accounts, receipts, checks, printed currency, circular letters, printed forms filled out in manuscript, and other printed material related to American finance, currency and lotteries during and after the Revolutionary War. Series I, Currency and Public Debt Documents, includes letters by Jacques-Pierre Brissot de Warville; Albert Gallatin; John Gibson; Alexander Hamilton; Gouverneur Morris; Robert Morris, and Thomas Willing. There is a small group of letters between Gouverneur Morris and the London bankers Sir Francis Baring, Edmund Boehm, and Thomas Henchman concerning their possible investment in the "new American funds" in 1790 and 1791. Printed material includes Thomas Paine's Decline and Fall of the English System of Finance (Philadelphia, 1796) as well as circular letters and debt payment tables. Other contents include reports on Revolutionary War loans and other contemporary financial matters; receipts issued by several states, particularly Connecticut and Massachusetts, between 1780 and 1789; and samples of state and Continental Congress paper money. Series II, United States Lottery Documents, contains letters by state agents for the Continental Lottery; accounts and account logs for the lottery; memoranda; and a printed lottery ticket from 1779.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: American Historical Financial Documents Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, accounts, receipts, checks, printed currency, circular letters, printed forms filled out in manuscript, and other printed material related to American finance, currency and lotteries during and after the Revolutionary War. Series I, Currency and Public Debt Documents, includes letters by Jacques-Pierre Brissot de Warville; Albert Gallatin; John Gibson; Alexander Hamilton; Gouverneur Morris; Robert Morris, and Thomas Willing. There is a small group of letters between Gouverneur Morris and the London bankers Sir Francis Baring, Edmund Boehm, and Thomas Henchman concerning their possible investment in the "new American funds" in 1790 and 1791. Printed material includes Thomas Paine's Decline and Fall of the English System of Finance (Philadelphia, 1796) as well as circular letters and debt payment tables. Other contents include reports on Revolutionary War loans and other contemporary financial matters; receipts issued by several states, particularly Connecticut and Massachusetts, between 1780 and 1789; and samples of state and Continental Congress paper money. Series II, United States Lottery Documents, contains letters by state agents for the Continental Lottery; accounts and account logs for the lottery; memoranda; and a printed lottery ticket from 1779.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from William Reese Co. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund and the Library Associates Fund, 2003.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: This collection was formed by the firm Donaldson, Lufkin, & Jenrette [DLJ]. and was known in the research community for a long period of time as the DLJ Collection.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series, both arranged in the order in which the material was received. Series I., Currency and Public Debt Documents, contains examples of currency and printed forms issued by various states and the Continental Congress, followed by alphabetically arranged correspondence, circular letters, official documents and printed ephemera. Series II, United States Lottery Documents, contains roughly chronologically arranged correspondence and other papers.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection consists of materials formerly classed as Uncat MSS 474 and Uncat MSS 516. In 2014, the collection received a baseline level of processing, including rehousing. The existing arrangement of material in each accession was retained. Folder titles were based on information provided by the bookdealer. Some names were updated to conform to Library of Congress name authorities.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The entire collection is available in digital form.

corpname.part: United States. Department of the Treasury

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence between Gouverneur Morris and the London banking partnership of Boehm, Baring and Henchman (Edmund Boehm, Sir Francis Baring, and Thomas Henchman) on a proposed investment in the "new American funds"

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Printed lottery ticket, class the third, with manuscript signature.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Printed lottery ticket, class the third, with manuscript signature.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Account of lottery money received in the loan office. Boston, Massachusetts.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Account of lottery money received in the loan office. Boston, Massachusetts.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Account of money received by Nicholas Gilman, commissioner of the loan office in New Hampshire, from the agent of the United States Lottery, for the sale of lottery tickets. [Concord, New Hampshire?]

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Account of money received by Nicholas Gilman, commissioner of the loan office in New Hampshire, from the agent of the United States Lottery, for the sale of lottery tickets. [Concord, New Hampshire?]

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from John Hartman, Pennsylvania agent of the Continental Lottery, to Sharp Delany and Joseph Bullock, managers of the lottery in Philadelphia. Reading, Pennsylvania.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from John Hartman, Pennsylvania agent of the Continental Lottery, to Sharp Delany and Joseph Bullock, managers of the lottery in Philadelphia. Reading, Pennsylvania.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Thomas Harwood, Continental loan officer for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany and Joseph Bullock, managers of the Continental Lottery. Annapolis, Maryland.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Thomas Harwood, Continental loan officer for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany and Joseph Bullock, managers of the Continental Lottery. Annapolis, Maryland.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, Continental Lottery winner, to the commissioners, seeking his prize. Middletown, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, Continental Lottery winner, to the commissioners, seeking his prize. Middletown, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, executor of the estate of a Connecticut lottery agent, to the managers of the Continental Lottery. Middletown, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, executor of the estate of a Connecticut lottery agent, to the managers of the Continental Lottery. Middletown, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, executor of the estate of a Connecticut lottery agent, to the managers of the Continental Lottery in Philadelphia. Middletown, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Wensley Hobby, executor of the estate of a Connecticut lottery agent, to the managers of the Continental Lottery in Philadelphia. Middletown, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from William Houston, delegate to the Continental Congress from New Jersey, to the managers of the Continental Lottery.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from William Houston, delegate to the Continental Congress from New Jersey, to the managers of the Continental Lottery.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: List of Continental Lottery agents to whom no credit was given via the sale of third class lottery tickets. [Philadelphia, Pennsylvania?]

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: List of Continental Lottery agents to whom no credit was given via the sale of third class lottery tickets. [Philadelphia, Pennsylvania?]

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Manuscript computations regarding the Continental Lottery.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Manuscript computations regarding the Continental Lottery.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Manuscript list and receipt for funds received from a Continental Lottery agent.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Manuscript list and receipt for funds received from a Continental Lottery agent.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Manuscript receipt to lottery agent Thomas Richardson for tickets sold in the third class of the Continental Lottery. [Annapolis, Maryland?]

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Manuscript receipt to lottery agent Thomas Richardson for tickets sold in the third class of the Continental Lottery. [Annapolis, Maryland?]

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Manuscript account of money paid in the loan office of Connecticut by several Continental Lottery agents. Hartford, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Manuscript account of money paid in the loan office of Connecticut by several Continental Lottery agents. Hartford, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Table of lottery tickets sold, recorded by Thomas Jett, agent of the lottery in Virginia.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Table of lottery tickets sold, recorded by Thomas Jett, agent of the lottery in Virginia.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Harwood, Thomas. Account for money received from lottery agents.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Harwood, Thomas. Account for money received from lottery agents.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Roderic Lawrence, agent of the Continental Lottery in Connecticut, to the managers of the lottery in Philadelphia. Hartford, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Roderic Lawrence, agent of the Continental Lottery in Connecticut, to the managers of the lottery in Philadelphia. Hartford, Connecticut.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Samuel Purviance, lottery agent for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany, manager of the Continental Lottery. Baltimore, Maryland.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Samuel Purviance, lottery agent for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany, manager of the Continental Lottery. Baltimore, Maryland.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Samuel Purviance, lottery agent for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany, manager of the Continental Lottery. Baltimore, Maryland.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Autograph letter, signed, from Samuel Purviance, lottery agent for Maryland, to Sharpe Delany, manager of the Continental Lottery. Baltimore, Maryland.

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Morris, Robert. Manuscript note regarding Robert Morris and the managers of the Continental Lottery. [Philadelphia, Pennsylvania?]

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Morris, Robert. Manuscript note regarding Robert Morris and the managers of the Continental Lottery. [Philadelphia, Pennsylvania?]

did.unittitle: Continental Lottery: Manuscript warrant, signed by Oliver Ellsworth as member of the Connecticut Governor's Council, commanding Hezekiah and Joshua Welles to appear before the county court of Hartford regarding a fraudulent lottery. Hartford, Connecticut.

descriptivenote.p: Continental Lottery: Manuscript warrant, signed by Oliver Ellsworth as member of the Connecticut Governor's Council, commanding Hezekiah and Joshua Welles to appear before the county court of Hartford regarding a fraudulent lottery. Hartford, Connecticut.
